title: Layered shadow border without extra DOM

requires: modern color syntax for color-mix() optional; static colors fallback provided
caveats: Subpixel shadow edges vary slightly by device pixel ratio.

fallback: Use fixed RGB shadows when color-mix() is unavailable.
fallback_test_method: @supports not (color: color-mix(in oklab, black, white)) rule keeps static shadow colors.
verification_evidence: Migrated example shows inset edge, lower divider, and outer lift as named checkpoints; browser smoke confirmed basic render with no horizontal overflow.
expected_primary_result: Card displays a soft outer lift and crisp inner highlight without border DOM.

title: Popover entry transition with @starting-style

requires: allow-discrete transition support for full display/overlay transition
caveats: Browsers without overlay/display discrete transitions still show and hide the popover without the full animation.

fallback: Popover remains functional; animation reduces to opacity/transform or instant open.
fallback_test_method: @supports blocks isolate transition-behavior and starting-style enhancements.
verification_evidence: Migrated example documents @starting-style and discrete-transition guard; browser smoke confirmed native popover open state and close control.
expected_primary_result: Popover fades and slides from a starting style with backdrop transition.
fallback_result: Popover opens natively with reduced or no transition.

title: Sequential sticky section headers with scroll-state queries

caveats: Older browsers keep the fallback sticky header styling and do not react to the stuck state.

fallback: Use neutral sticky section headers with persistent dividers.
fallback_test_method: Base sticky styling works before @container scroll-state() enhancement.
verification_evidence: Migrated example keeps fixed-height sticky headers, adds technique checkpoints, and browser scroll smoke confirmed sequential stuck-state styling without overflow.
expected_primary_result: Each section header changes color, shadow, and descendant transform only while it is stuck, then the next sticky header replaces it.
fallback_result: Section headers remain sticky with persistent subtle dividers.

title: Component layout with container queries

caveats: Container queries respond to the component container, not the viewport.

fallback: Use a single-column card that remains readable without @container.
fallback_test_method: Base card layout is single-column before @container enhancement.
verification_evidence: Migrated example includes a resizable component frame; browser smoke confirmed narrow and wide container grid changes.
expected_primary_result: Card reorganizes based on its own container width.

title: Tooltip popover positioned with CSS anchors

caveats: Anchor positioning is still uneven across browser channels; fallback uses fixed viewport placement.

fallback: Use fixed popover placement and preserve the native trigger relationship.
fallback_test_method: @supports (anchor-name: --x) applies enhanced placement only when available.
verification_mode: interactive browser smoke with fallback inspection
verification_evidence: Migrated example opens as a native popover with fixed fallback placement; browser smoke confirmed popover visibility and anchored trigger naming.
expected_primary_result: Popover tooltip tracks the anchored trigger and can try alternate positions.
fallback_result: Popover appears in a fixed reachable viewport position with native open/close behavior.

title: Fluid type driven by container inline size

caveats: Use rem bounds so text does not become unreadably small or large.

fallback: Use fixed readable type sizes if container query units are unavailable.
fallback_test_method: Base heading uses fixed rem sizing before @supports (font-size: 1cqi) applies.
verification_evidence: Migrated example uses fixed readable fallback type plus cqi enhancement; browser smoke confirmed font size changes with container width.
expected_primary_result: Heading scales with its container rather than the viewport.
fallback_result: Heading uses fixed readable rem sizing.

title: CSS if() branch with static fallback

requires: browser channel with CSS if() support
caveats: Syntax and support are experimental; keep production behavior outside the if() branch until target support is confirmed.

fallback: Set the production visual result with normal custom properties before the experimental branch.
fallback_test_method: Base custom-property values render success and warning cards when if() is ignored.
a11y_checks: no behavior depends on experimental CSS
verification_evidence: Migrated example keeps success and warning fallback cards complete while isolating experimental if() syntax behind @supports.
expected_primary_result: Supporting browsers can choose tone styles through CSS if().
fallback_result: Non-supporting browsers use explicit fallback custom-property values for both tones.

title: Sequential animation with indexed custom properties

caveats: Respect reduced motion and avoid delaying critical content.

fallback: Disable animation under prefers-reduced-motion while preserving content order.
verification_evidence: Migrated example shows indexed --i delays, reduced-motion guard, and readable content order.
expected_primary_result: Rows reveal in order with calculated delays.
fallback_result: Rows render immediately with no motion.

title: Progressive sidenotes with anchor()

caveats: Sidenotes should fall back to inline notes on narrow screens and unsupported browsers.

fallback: Render notes inline after the paragraph before promoting them to the margin.
fallback_test_method: Base HTML displays notes in document flow; @supports and wide-container media query promote them.
a11y_checks: sup marker has aria-hidden text alternative nearby
verification_evidence: Migrated example keeps the sidenote inline by default and documents the wide anchor() promotion path.
expected_primary_result: Wide layouts position sidenotes in the margin aligned to their anchors.
fallback_result: Notes remain inline below their paragraph.

title: Previous-sibling styling with :has(+ ...)

caveats: Avoid overly broad :has() selectors on very large dynamic DOMs.

fallback: Style only the target item directly; previous-sibling accent is omitted when :has() is unsupported.
fallback_test_method: @supports selector(li:has(+ li)) applies the relational enhancement only when available.
verification_evidence: Migrated example scopes li:has(+ .current) to one adjacent step; browser smoke confirmed previous-step styling.
expected_primary_result: The item before the current step receives a completed-state accent without extra classes.
fallback_result: Only the current step is highlighted.

title: Cross-document navigation with @view-transition

requires: user-initiated push, replace, or traverse navigation
caveats: MDN marks @view-transition as Limited availability; unsupported browsers perform normal navigation.

fallback: Use ordinary same-origin links; navigation remains functional without transition animation.
fallback_test_method: Both pages are standalone documents and links work without @view-transition support.
verification_evidence: Migrated two-page example keeps real links and repeated @view-transition opt-in; browser smoke confirmed opt-in and detail href.
expected_primary_result: Same-origin navigation animates old and new root snapshots with custom keyframes.
fallback_result: Links navigate normally with no animation.

title: Scroll-driven progress counter label

requires: container query length units such as cqh
caveats: MDN marks animation-timeline as Limited availability; unsupported browsers keep the static fallback label.
Generated counter text is visual; keep meaningful progress text in real markup for accessibility.

fallback: Render a static progress label and track before the scroll-timeline enhancement; do not use generated counter text for critical status.
fallback_test_method: Base markup shows the rail, label, and static 0% complete text before the @supports enhancement applies.
a11y_checks: real explanatory text remains in markup
verification_evidence: Example registers --complete as an integer, drives it from the root scroll timeline, feeds counter-reset, and browser smoke confirmed the label reaches 100% while staying inside the rail.
expected_primary_result: The visual label counts from 0% to 100% and travels down the rail as the page scrolls.
fallback_result: The rail and static 0% complete label remain visible without scroll-driven animation support.

title: Mobile safe-area shell with env()

requires: safe-area-inset-* environment variables for non-zero device insets
viewport-fit=cover when opting into edge-to-edge layout
caveats: Desktop browsers and devices without cutouts usually resolve safe-area-inset-* to 0.
Safe-area insets describe occupied system UI space, not desired visual margins, so add normal spacing around them.
safe-area-max-inset-* is intentionally not used because support is narrower.

fallback: Use normal spacing first; env() additions resolve to 0 or fallback values when no safe area is exposed.
fallback_test_method: Base padding and fixed action offsets are declared before env()/max() enhancements and remain usable with zero simulated insets.
a11y_checks: controls remain inside safe content area
verification_evidence: Reconstructed example combines viewport-fit=cover with safe-area padding variables and a simulated device frame; browser smoke confirmed responsive render, no horizontal overflow, and no action overlap.
expected_primary_result: Shell padding and floating actions sit inside the safe content area while preserving normal visual spacing.
fallback_result: Shell uses ordinary 16px spacing and remains readable when safe-area values are 0 or unsupported.

title: Animated gradient text shine

caveats: Do not make text transparent until a background-clip:text support query applies the enhancement.
Animated shine is decorative and should stop or become static for reduced-motion users.
Gradient stops can create low-contrast phases; test the readable fallback and the animated state.

fallback: Render ordinary readable text color first; enhance to clipped gradient text only inside @supports and freeze the shine under prefers-reduced-motion.
fallback_test_method: Base .shine-text color is visible before @supports; @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) removes the animation and leaves a static gradient.
a11y_checks: effect is decorative and not the only status cue
verification_evidence: Reconstructed example keeps real text readable before @supports, applies gradient clipping with a corrected @keyframes rule, and includes a reduced-motion stop rule.
expected_primary_result: Supported browsers render a moving highlight across the text fill without layout shift.
fallback_result: Text remains readable as a normal solid color when clipping or animation is unavailable.

title: Relative color token palette

requires: modern CSS color functions such as rgb(from ...) and oklch(from ...)
caveats: Keep absolute fallback tokens before the relative-color enhancement for older browsers.
Relative color relationships do not guarantee accessible contrast for arbitrary source colors.
Use literal colors rather than custom properties in @supports tests; assign custom-property origins inside the guarded block.

fallback: Declare absolute fallback tokens for strong, soft, border, shadow, and accent colors before replacing them with relative colors inside @supports.
fallback_test_method: Base token values are valid without relative syntax; @supports (color: oklch(from red l c h)) switches cards to derived tokens.
a11y_checks: generated colors checked as decorative token relationships
verification_evidence: Reconstructed example keeps absolute fallback tokens for three tones, then derives strong, soft, border, shadow, and accent values with relative color syntax under @supports.
expected_primary_result: Supported browsers derive coherent component tokens from each card's --tone without extra markup or JavaScript.
fallback_result: Cards use absolute fallback color tokens and remain readable without relative color support.

title: Animated conic gradient border

caveats: The animated border is decorative; stop rotation for reduced-motion users.
The card face needs an opaque background so the gradient does not reduce content readability.
Use this for featured surfaces rather than dense repeated lists.

fallback: Use a static border and solid card face first; supporting browsers rotate a registered angle custom property in the conic gradient.
fallback_test_method: The card has a normal border/background before pseudo-element animation; @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) disables the spin.
verification_evidence: Reconstructed example registers --border-angle, paints conic-gradient pseudo-elements behind an opaque card, and disables animation under reduced motion.
expected_primary_result: A conic gradient rotates smoothly around a featured card while the content remains readable.
fallback_result: The card keeps a readable static border and solid face.

title: Grid height transition without max-height

requires: target-browser support for the 0fr to 1fr grid track transition pattern
caveats: MDN support for grid-template-rows does not by itself prove 0fr to 1fr interpolation; verify this transition in target browsers before production.
The inner wrapper must hide overflow while the grid row transitions.
Remove the transition for reduced-motion users.

fallback: Without transition support the disclosure opens and closes instantly; without JavaScript the initial panel remains expanded and readable.
fallback_test_method: The button toggles aria-expanded and grid-template-rows between 0fr and 1fr, reduced-motion removes the transition, and the no-JS state remains expanded.
a11y_checks: collapsed content gets aria-hidden and inert
verification_evidence: Reconstructed example uses a button, aria-expanded/aria-controls state sync, grid-template-rows 0fr/1fr, an overflow-hidden inner wrapper, and a reduced-motion guard.
expected_primary_result: The panel animates to its natural content height without max-height timing drift.
fallback_result: The panel opens or closes instantly when the transition is unavailable, and remains expanded and readable when JavaScript is unavailable.

title: Column-width Masonry Feed

requires: break-inside: avoid to keep cards from fragmenting
caveats: Visual reading flow is column-major and can differ from row-wise masonry expectations.
Keyboard and screen-reader order follow DOM order, so use this only when that order remains clear.
This is not experimental CSS Grid masonry; do not treat it as support for grid-lane masonry.

fallback: Render the feed as a normal one-column list first; supporting browsers enhance it with column-width and unsplit cards.
fallback_test_method: Base .masonry-feed uses display:grid with a single-column gap; @supports (column-width: 16rem) switches to multi-column layout.
verification_evidence: Runnable example starts as a one-column list, then uses column-width, column-gap, inline-block cards, and break-inside: avoid; browser smoke confirmed desktop generated columns and mobile single-column layout without overflow.
expected_primary_result: Cards of varied height pack into responsive generated columns without JavaScript measurement or absolute positioning.
fallback_result: Cards remain a readable single-column ordered list.

title: Stepped fluid sizing with round()

requires: CSS round() stepped value math function
caveats: Use round() on fluid values or intervals that can actually vary; hardcoding both sides is redundant.
Unsupported browsers drop the rounded declaration, so keep the unrounded clamp() value first.
If adapting the article's container-query-unit examples, also check the support target for cqi/cqw in your browser matrix.

fallback: Declare the normal fluid clamp() value first, then override only inside @supports with a rounded value snapped to a rem or px step.
fallback_test_method: The example keeps unrounded clamp() variables and visible comparison cards before @supports (font-size: round(1rem, 1px)) applies the stepped enhancement.
states_demonstrated: spacing and media size snapped to intervals
a11y_checks: real text remains readable in fallback and enhanced states
effect is layout rhythm only and not a status cue
verification_evidence: Reconstructed example compares unrounded clamp() sizing with a round()-enhanced card that snaps title, gap, padding, and media size to predictable steps.
expected_primary_result: Supported browsers keep fluid sizing but snap typography, spacing, and media dimensions to predictable intervals.
fallback_result: Cards remain fluid and readable with the original clamp() values when round() is unsupported.
